title: Add data validation and range constraints for vital signs
body:
## Problem
Vital sign inputs lack validation and accept unrealistic values. VitalsTracker allows any numeric value for temperature, heart rate, blood pressure, and weight without checking against physiologically valid ranges. This can result in impossible health data being stored (e.g., 500 bpm heart rate, -50F temperature).

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/VitalsTracker.tsx:L52-L112` — Input fields use basic `<input type="number">` with no range constraints or validation before submission
- `src/main/db/repos/health.ts:L37-L75` — No validation layer in the `add()` method; data is inserted directly into database
- `src/renderer/src/hooks/useHealth.ts:L40-L52` — Mutation function passes data through without validation

## Expected
- Temperature: 95–104F (35–40C)
- Heart rate: 40–200 bpm
- Blood pressure: systolic 70–180, diastolic 40–120 mmHg
- Weight: 50–600 lbs based on unit
- All fields should reject values outside safe ranges with user-facing error messages

## Proposed fix
Add a validation schema in both frontend (React component) and backend (healthRepo.add) using Zod or similar. Display inline validation messages when user enters invalid values. Show a warning toast if data is outside normal ranges but still valid (e.g., "Unusually high BP").

## Impact
Prevents garbage data from corrupting health records and charting. Improves data integrity and user trust in the system.

title: Implement edit functionality for health logs and medications
body:
## Problem
Users can only delete health logs and medications; there is no edit capability. If a user enters data incorrectly (typo in symptoms, wrong dosage), they must delete the entire record and re-enter it. This is a significant UX friction point for daily health tracking.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/HealthTimeline.tsx:L65-L70` — Only delete button shown on logs, no edit option
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/MedicationTracker.tsx:L143-L148` — Medication list shows delete button in hover state but no edit
- `src/main/db/repos/health.ts:L73-L75` — No `update()` method defined
- `src/main/db/repos/medications.ts:L44-L50` — Only `setActive()` exists; no general update method
- `src/main/ipc/dbIpc.ts:L17-L18` — IPC allowlist for health and medications repos does not include update methods

## Expected
Clicking an edit button on a log/medication opens a modal pre-populated with existing values. User can modify any field and save changes. Backend supports `update()` method that modifies the record without creating duplicates.

## Proposed fix
1. Add `update(id, fields)` method to `healthRepo` and `medicationsRepo`
2. Create edit modal component (similar to add form) that accepts initialValues
3. Add edit button to HealthTimeline and MedicationTracker UI
4. Add 'update' to ALLOWED methods in dbIpc.ts
5. Create `useUpdateHealthLog()` and `useUpdateMedication()` hooks

## Impact
Major UX improvement; users can correct mistakes without losing history. Increases adoption and daily usage frequency.

title: Add data export/import functionality for health records
body:
## Problem
There is no way to export or import health data. Users cannot back up their records, migrate to another app, or bulk-upload historical data from prior tracking systems. This creates a lock-in concern and prevents data portability.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/HealthHome.tsx` — Home page is a stub; no export/import UI exists
- `src/main/db/repos/health.ts` — No export-related methods (e.g., `exportCsv()`, `exportJson()`)
- `src/main/db/repos/medications.ts` — Same limitation
- No IPC handlers for file I/O in health context

## Expected
Users can:
- Export all health logs as CSV or JSON
- Export medications list
- Import CSV/JSON files to bulk-add records
- Preview imported data before committing

## Proposed fix
1. Add `exportCsv()` and `exportJson()` methods to health and medications repos
2. Add corresponding IPC handlers and frontend endpoints
3. Create export/import modal UI in HealthHome
4. Add Electron dialog for file selection (save/open)
5. Validate imported data against schema before insertion

## Impact
Enables data portability, user confidence, and integration with other health platforms (Apple Health, Google Fit).

title: Add charting and trend analysis for vital signs
body:
## Problem
Health data is collected but minimally visualized. The sparkline charts in HealthTimeline are too small to show trends effectively. No statistical analysis (averages, moving averages, trend lines) is available. Users cannot identify patterns (e.g., "my BP is trending up", "sleep quality improving").

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/HealthTimeline.tsx:L78-L94` — Chart component uses Sparkline for 5-metric overview; charts are 120x24px, not interactive
- `src/renderer/src/shared/Sparkline` — No date labels, tooltips, or zoom capability
- No larger charting library integrated (e.g., recharts, Chart.js)
- No statistical endpoints for min/max/avg/stddev calculations

## Expected
- Full-size interactive charts for each metric over time ranges (7d, 30d, 90d, 1y)
- Trend lines showing moving averages
- Comparison metrics (e.g., "Your avg HR this month: 72 bpm, vs. 70 bpm last month")
- Correlation detection (e.g., "Your mood and sleep quality are 0.81 correlated")
- Ability to click data points for detail view

## Proposed fix
1. Add recharts or similar library for interactive charting
2. Create `ChartView` component with tab for each vital
3. Add backend aggregation methods (`getStats(metric, days)`) to healthRepo
4. Include date filtering UI (date range picker)
5. Optional: Add chart export (PNG) capability

## Impact
Makes health data actionable; users can identify patterns and take corrective action. Significantly increases engagement.

title: Add medication interaction warnings and drug database
body:
## Problem
Medications are tracked but with no drug interaction checking. Users adding multiple medications get no warnings if those drugs interact negatively, which is a safety concern. Dosages are free-text and not validated against standard drug formats.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/MedicationTracker.tsx:L60-L89` — Dosage field is unstructured text input (`placeholder="Dosage"`)
- `src/main/db/repos/medications.ts:L23-L42` — No validation of dosage format or interaction checking
- No external drug database integration (RxNorm, DrugBank, FDA)
- Frequency field is also free-text

## Expected
- Dosage field accepts structured input: number + unit + frequency (e.g., "500mg twice daily")
- When user adds a medication, system checks against existing medications for known interactions
- Display warning modal if interaction detected: "Aspirin + Warfarin: Increased bleeding risk"
- Optional: Link to drug information pages (FDA, Mayo Clinic)
- Frequency selector (daily, twice daily, 3x/week, as needed, etc.)

## Proposed fix
1. Integrate with RxNorm API or create a curated drug database
2. Replace free-text dosage/frequency inputs with structured selectors
3. Add interaction-checking service in `src/main/services/health/` (or similar)
4. Display warnings in modal before save
5. Add disclaimer: "Not a substitute for medical advice"

## Impact
Major safety feature; prevents accidental harmful drug combinations. Increases user trust and liability protection.

title: Add HIPAA/privacy disclaimers and data encryption at rest
body:
## Problem
Health data is sensitive PHI (Protected Health Information). The app stores medication names, symptoms, and vital signs in plaintext SQLite without user consent disclaimers or encryption. There is no privacy policy or warning that data is not encrypted by default.

## Evidence
- `src/main/db/schema.ts:L68-L101` — health_logs and medications tables contain plaintext health data
- No encryption layer between application and database file
- No HIPAA/privacy disclaimers in health module UI
- No user consent prompt before first health entry
- Database file location is standard Electron userData path (not encrypted)

## Expected
- First-time prompt: "Warning: Health data is stored locally on this device. Not HIPAA-compliant." with acknowledgment checkbox
- Persistent disclaimer in health module header
- Documentation: Privacy policy, data retention, export/delete options
- Option to enable SQLite encryption (sqlcipher or similar)
- Clear labeling: "For personal tracking only, not medical advice"

## Proposed fix
1. Add privacy modal on first health module access (store flag in user_settings)
2. Add disclaimer banner in HealthHome or module header
3. Integrate sqlcipher for encrypted database (optional feature)
4. Update schema to support encryption metadata
5. Add privacy docs to README or in-app help

## Impact
Legal/compliance protection; user trust. Essential for health apps to disclose limitations and data handling.

title: Missing multi-user support for shared household health tracking
body:
## Problem
Health module assumes single-user tracking. If multiple household members want to track health in the same Daja instance, there is no way to separate their data or assign entries to individuals. All logs are in a single table with no user_id field.

## Evidence
- `src/main/db/schema.ts:L68-L87` — health_logs table has no user_id or user_name column
- `src/main/db/repos/health.ts` — No filtering by user in list() or recent() methods
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/HealthHome.tsx` — No user selector or dropdown visible
- IPC calls in `useHealth.ts` do not pass user context

## Expected
Optional multi-user mode:
- Add user_id field to health_logs and medications tables
- Add user selector dropdown in HealthHome and HealthTimeline
- Filter data by selected user in UI and queries
- Medications assigned to specific users (e.g., "Mom's aspirin", "Dad's metformin")
- Option in settings to enable/disable multi-user mode

## Proposed fix
1. Add user_id INTEGER column to health_logs and medications tables (nullable for backward compat)
2. Add `get_current_user()` context hook (or hardcode default user initially)
3. Update repo queries to filter by user_id if set
4. Add user selector UI component in health modules
5. Add user management page in app settings

## Impact
Enables family health tracking; increases use case diversity. Lower priority but high UX value for multi-user homes.

title: Implement temperature/weight unit conversion and default preferences
body:
## Problem
Temperature and weight have unit selectors on each form (F/C, lbs/kg), but no global user preference is saved. Users must re-select units every time they log vitals. Charts show weights without unit context. No automatic conversion between units for comparison.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/VitalsTracker.tsx:L60-L68` — Temperature unit selector on form, no saved preference
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/VitalsTracker.tsx:L103-L111` — Weight unit selector, same issue
- `src/main/db/schema.ts:L73-L80` — temperature_unit and weight_unit stored per log, no global default
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/HealthTimeline.tsx:L28, L59` — Weight chart shows no unit label

## Expected
- User settings page option: "Preferred temperature unit: F / C"
- User settings page option: "Preferred weight unit: lbs / kg"
- Form inputs pre-fill with saved preference
- Charts display unit label (e.g., "Weight (lbs)")
- If data logged in different unit, auto-convert for comparison (show both on hover)

## Proposed fix
1. Add temp_unit and weight_unit fields to user_settings table
2. Create settings hook `useHealthPreferences()` that reads/writes user_settings
3. Pre-fill VitalsTracker unit selectors from preferences
4. Update HealthTimeline Chart to display unit labels
5. Add optional unit conversion utility for historical data comparison

## Impact
Small but important polish; reduces friction and improves consistency for daily logging.

title: Add accessibility labels and keyboard navigation for health forms
body:
## Problem
Health module forms lack proper ARIA labels, semantic structure, and keyboard navigation support. Screen reader users cannot understand form sections. Tab order is unclear. Slider controls (mood, energy, severity) are not accessible to keyboard-only users.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/VitalsTracker.tsx:L42-L127` — Input fields use `<label>` but no htmlFor linking; Field component has no aria-describedby
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/SymptomLogger.tsx:L160-L176` — Range slider has no aria-label, aria-valuetext, or keyboard event handlers
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/MedicationTracker.tsx:L116-L150` — Medication list items not semantic (no ul/li); toggle button lacks aria-pressed
- No focus management or focus trap in modals (future edit modal)

## Expected
- All inputs have `htmlFor` connected labels
- Range sliders have `aria-label`, `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, `aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuetext`
- Sliders respond to arrow keys (Left/Right, Up/Down)
- Lists use semantic `<ul>` / `<li>`
- Buttons have descriptive text or aria-label (e.g., Trash2 icon button: `aria-label="Delete medication"`)
- Color not sole indicator (e.g., active medication toggle needs text or icon change)

## Proposed fix
1. Refactor Slider component to handle keyboard events (ArrowUp/Down) and add ARIA
2. Add htmlFor to all Field labels
3. Convert medication list to semantic HTML
4. Add aria-label to icon buttons
5. Test with screen reader (NVDA, JAWS) and keyboard navigation

## Impact
Makes health tracking accessible to users with disabilities; legal compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA).

title: Add reminder notifications for medication adherence and checkups
body:
## Problem
Medications and health goals have no reminder system. Users who start a daily medication have no notification to take it. Regular checkups (e.g., "log vitals weekly") are not tracked. This reduces adherence and defeats the purpose of tracking.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/MedicationTracker.tsx` — No reminder UI or time-based fields
- `src/main/db/schema.ts:L89-L101` — medications table has no reminder_time, reminder_enabled, or next_dose fields
- No notification service integrated in health context
- IPC handlers do not include reminder methods

## Expected
- Medication form includes: "Send reminder?" checkbox, time of day (e.g., 8am, 2pm)
- Desktop notifications at specified times: "Take your aspirin"
- Weekly/monthly check-in reminders: "Log your vitals this week"
- Medication adherence tracker: Show streak of consecutive days taken
- Optional: Snooze reminder for 1 hour

## Proposed fix
1. Add reminder_enabled, reminder_time, reminder_frequency columns to medications table
2. Create reminder service in `src/main/services/health/` with schedule logic
3. Use Electron's app.setLoginItemSettings() or a cron-like scheduler for background reminders
4. Add notification UI in medication form
5. Add adherence counter component to MedicationTracker

## Impact
Significantly improves medication adherence; encourages regular logging habits. High-value feature for compliance-focused users.

title: Add AI health coaching integration and symptom analysis
body:
## Problem
Collected health data sits static with no insights or guidance. Users log symptoms and vitals but receive no AI-powered analysis, suggestions, or coaching. The app mentions "AI coaching integration" in scope but has no implementation. No integration with Claude or other LLM for health insights.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/health/HealthHome.tsx:L3-L6` — Placeholder text: "Health Tracker — wiring in Phase 6"
- No AI conversation handlers in db repos or services
- IPC allowlist includes 'conversations' repo but no health-specific coaching methods
- No health context fields in ai_conversations table (schema.ts:L133-L147)
- useRecentHealth hook exists but not used anywhere (src/renderer/src/hooks/useHealth.ts:L32-L38)

## Expected
- "Ask AI Coach" button in health modules
- Sample prompts: "Analyze my sleep trends", "Is my blood pressure rising?", "Suggest wellness goals based on my logs"
- AI response explains patterns and recommends actions (e.g., "Your mood is 0.8 correlated with sleep; try 8 hours")
- Store conversation history per user
- Optional: Voice input for health coaching (dictate symptoms, get analysis)
- Disclaimer: "AI insights are informational only, not medical advice"

## Proposed fix
1. Create `useHealthCoach()` hook that calls Claude API with recent health context
2. Add "Health Coach" button and conversation modal to HealthHome
3. Implement prompt engineering: Include last 30 days of data in context, ask for specific analysis
4. Store conversations in ai_conversations table with module='health' and context_ticker='health'
5. Add response parsing to extract actionable insights (goals, warnings)
6. Add privacy/liability disclaimers prominently

## Impact
Transforms raw data into actionable health insights; major engagement booster. Differentiates Daja from simple tracking apps.
